title: Golang panic since upgrade from 3.0.2 to 3.2.3
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Following upgrade from podman v3.0.2 to v3.2.3 in RHEL8.4, we are unable to use it again, we have a go panic message.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Upgrade to 3.2.3

2. 

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
[root@server:~]$ podman run test
panic: runtime error: slice bounds out of range [:2] with length 1

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman should create and start a container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Thu Jul 29 17:02:43 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Virtual machine running under VMware.

comments.body:
It looks like podman doesn't like this kind of configuration:
```
[registries.block]
registries = ['*']
```
It crashes.
Switching to:
```
[registries.block]
registries = []
```
Solved the issue.

Thanks for reaching out and for providing the details!

@lsm5 PTAL,  https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/pkg/sysregistriesv2/system_registries_v2.go#L83 is causing the issue.
Should be addressed by https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1368 . @vrothberg Can we transfer this issue to c/image?

> Should be addressed by [containers/image#1368](https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1368) . @vrothberg Can we transfer this issue to c/image?

btw, the PR is still being worked on. Will be vendored into podman once merged.

> Should be addressed by [containers/image#1368](https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1368) . @vrothberg Can we transfer this issue to c/image?

I think we should keep it here and close only once it has been merged.

This looks larger than https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1368 ; basically every use of `[:2]` and `[0:2]` in that code (and maybe more checks throughout the codebase?) should be replaced with `strings.HasPrefix`.
… Looking at `git grep '\[[0-9:]\+\]'`, nothing else in c/image seems very suspicious, but I haven’t audited every single indexing operation carefully.

> It looks like podman doesn't like this kind of configuration:
> 
> ```
> [registries.block]
> registries = ['*']
> ```
> 
> It crashes. Switching to:
> 
> ```
> [registries.block]
> registries = []
> ```
> 
> Solved the issue.

FWIW the former configuration does, AFAICT, nothing at all; the `*` entry does not match anything. So replacing the configuration with the latter empty one is the right thing to do, though of course Podman should not be crashing.
@mtrmac thanks a lot for the pointers above!

> … Looking at `git grep '\[[0-9:]\+\]'`, nothing else in c/image seems very suspicious, but I haven’t audited every single indexing operation carefully.

I found one more instance, and I have now filed https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1396 .
Closing since it has been fixed.
